audit_items.proof_trace: All explicit frees in helloworld.c follow the use-before-free pattern: RedisModule_FreeString/FreeCallReply/CloseKey are always called after the last access to the pointer. Specifically, in HelloListSplice (L166-167) ListPush uses `ele` before FreeString; in HelloZsumRange (L397-400, L407-410) the loop body completes before FreeString; the StringDMA pointer `s` in HelloToggleCase is never accessed after CloseKey. Functions using AutoMemory (HelloListSpliceAuto, HelloRepl1/2, HelloHCopy, HelloLeftPad) have no explicit frees and no UAF risk.
